title: cerastium gracile subsp. ramosissimum
intro: Cerastium gracile subsp. ramosissimum, also known as a type of creeping or spreading mouse-ear chickweed, is a perennial plant species within the carnation family. It has been found to have medicinal properties, particularly in its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which are attributed to the presence of flavonoids and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of this plant include its potential to alleviate skin issues, such as eczema and acne, as well as its anti-inflammatory properties, which may help reduce swelling and pain. The plant is traditionally prepared as a tincture, infusion, or ointment for topical use, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for medicinal purposes.
